verified_fixes: ProgressBar inner setTimeout leak (Carlos QA#70 fix) — VERIFIED ✅
useTimeTick perf leak (Carlos QA#69 fix) — VERIFIED ✅
Idle dual-render conference+wander (Carlos QA#69 fix) — VERIFIED ✅
carry_overs: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)
activityLog.filter() called 3× inline per render — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)
Hover cards inaccessible on touch devices — STRIKE 2 (Maya)
WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)
Character name labels 0.44rem unreadable on mobile — STRIKE 2 (Maya)
103 timer instances with complex interdependencies — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)
Worker filter buttons overflow on mobile — STRIKE 2 (Maya)
Idle micro-bubble Date.now() expression in render — STRIKE 2 (Carlos)

suggestions.title: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions
suggestions.description: pingReaction={null} is hardcoded on the Bloberto character component. The sonar ping animation (char--pinged, sonar-ping keyframe) is fully implemented in CSS but can never trigger on Bloberto because the prop is always null. This breaks the click-to-interact feature for the main character. Fix: wire pingReaction state to Bloberto the same way it's wired to workers.

suggestions.title: activityLog.filter() called 3× inline per render — not memoized
suggestions.description: The activity log section runs three separate .filter() passes on the full activityLog array inline in JSX every render. With a growing log (100+ entries), this is wasteful. Extract into a single useMemo that returns { filteredByType, filteredByWorker, combinedFiltered } and only recomputes when activityLog, activeFilter, or workerFilter changes.

suggestions.title: Hover cards completely inaccessible on touch devices
suggestions.description: The char-hover-card and desk monitor tooltips rely entirely on :hover CSS which doesn't work on mobile. The CSS has a @media (hover: none) rule for vacant desk cards but not for worker hover cards. Touch users can't see worker details (task, progress, role). Need: onClick toggle for hover cards on touch devices, or use the existing click-to-inspect mechanism to show a persistent card.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk
suggestions.description: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES is a const array defined around line 543 but referenced earlier in the component. JavaScript hoists const declarations but not their initialization — accessing before the declaration line throws a ReferenceError. Currently works by coincidence (the reference is inside a useEffect that runs after mount), but any refactor that moves it could cause a crash. Move all const arrays to the top of the file or to a constants module.

suggestions.title: Footer quote cycling silently stops at end of array
suggestions.description: The footer tagline cycles through TAGLINES array but when it reaches the end, it stays on the last item forever. No wrap-around, no visual indication. Fix: modulo the index (idx % TAGLINES.length) to cycle infinitely, or add a subtle 'refresh' button.

suggestions.title: Character name labels at 0.44rem unreadable on mobile
suggestions.description: At 480px viewport width, .char__name is set to 0.45rem (about 7px). This is below the minimum readable size for most users. Bump to at least 0.55rem on mobile, or hide names entirely and rely on hover/tap cards.

suggestions.title: App.jsx and Office.jsx are monolith files (1432 and 1660 lines)
suggestions.description: Both files exceed 1400 lines with deeply nested JSX. Extract: ActivityLog component, VibeDashboard component, WorkerCard component, ConferenceTable component. This is a refactor debt item — not blocking but making every future change harder.

suggestions.title: 103 timer instances with complex interdependencies
suggestions.description: Counted 103 setTimeout/setInterval calls across the codebase. Many have cleanup in useEffect returns, but the interaction between timers (e.g., speech bubble timeout vs poll interval vs animation timers) creates subtle state races. Consider a centralized timer manager or at minimum document the timer dependency graph.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use inline styles instead of CSS classes
suggestions.description: Toast notifications for worker completion and plant milestones use inline style objects in JSX instead of CSS classes. This makes them un-themeable and inconsistent with the rest of the app's CSS-driven theming. Extract to .completion-toast and .plant-toast CSS classes.

suggestions.title: Worker filter buttons overflow on narrow mobile screens
suggestions.description: Activity log filter bar renders a button per worker inline. On 375px with 5+ workers, buttons overflow or wrap awkwardly. Add flex-wrap: wrap with proper gap, or switch to a <select> dropdown on mobile.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble Date.now() expression in render is illegible
suggestions.description: Line ~786: complex inline expression using Date.now() with charCode hashing runs on every render. Extract to getIdleBubble(workerId, tick) utility function.

suggestions.title: Vibe constants scattered across 3 files
suggestions.description: VIBE_RANK, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_HISTORY_KEY in App.jsx; BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS in Office.jsx; VIBE_WHITEBOARD in constants.js. Consolidate to a single vibe constants file.

suggestions.title: Dashboard solo worker card layout jump
suggestions.description: When transitioning from 2+ workers to 1, the solo card layout doesn't activate until fading workers complete (400ms delay), causing a visual jump from left-aligned to centered.

suggestions.title: No deep-linking to specific worker card
suggestions.description: Tab deep-linking via hash works (#office, #dashboard) but can't link to a specific worker. The focusedWorker mechanism exists — extend it to support ?worker=sofia in URL.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline re-reads localStorage redundantly
suggestions.description: On every vibe change, localStorage.getItem + JSON.parse runs even though the data is already in React state (vibeHistory). Use state as source of truth, only write to localStorage.

summary: QA Review #72: No code changes since review #71 — all 16 suggestions carry over to strike 2. Carlos's QA#69-70 fixes (useTimeTick, idle dual-render, standup dual-render, ProgressBar leak) remain verified and solid. The top 5 HIGH items are unchanged: Bloberto ping exclusion, unmemoized activity filters, touch-inaccessible hover cards, TDZ risk, and footer cycling dead-end. All are small-effort fixes that would meaningfully improve the product. The codebase is feature-rich and genuinely fun (fun_factor: 5/5) but accumulating tech debt — the monolith files and timer complexity make every change riskier. Score holds at 3.5/5 until the HIGH carry-overs get addressed.
